The other thing is no current prismata unit has a "per 2" or similar wording, so we don't have to worry about integer division. So this is what I'm looking at before I even start assessing balance.

[2018-03-26 13:09] masn6811 (Masne98888): Now, as far as balance goes, :Green: :Blue: :Red: :Red: for a 4 health swing is a really good ratio. It's so good that it even makes buying engineers for this quite worth, and provides a gold sink in high econ.
[2018-03-26 13:09] masn6811 (Masne98888): it also gains the 'electrovore advantage' letting you sac one engi and buy another, basically husk buying rights, which is really strong
[2018-03-26 13:10] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): It just autospends leftover GBRR
[2018-03-26 13:10] masn6811 (Masne98888): as far as pure power level in a set alone, its probably fine, but in conjection with caut, antima, etc, i think i could be opressive
[2018-03-26 13:10] masn6811 (Masne98888): since it doesnt actually use the energy, it allows for "using the engineer twice"
[2018-03-26 13:11] masn6811 (Masne98888): you can conceivably use the engineer "three times" between vore/caut, tesla, and this. and the thing about this is its acts as a tech sink
[2018-03-26 13:12] masn6811 (Masne98888): like the main reason tesla + vore/caut isnt often a thing is you dont want CBA, since that gets you overteched (esp buying a new engi every turn), but this solves that problem
[2018-03-26 13:12] masn6811 (Masne98888): as far as spiritfryer's suggestion goes, as i mentioned earlier, it would make the unit ordering dependant. so don't do that
[2018-03-26 13:13] masn6811 (Masne98888): my concern with this unit is that it seems impossible to balance, in the sense that it will either be too good with the synergy unit/sets, or useless otherwise
[2018-03-26 13:13] masn6811 (Masne98888): so that's all i have for now. i hope my feedback was informative
[2018-03-26 13:15] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Pay now is 1GBRRe, looks complex.
[2018-03-26 13:16] masn6811 (Masne98888): requiring you have engies and paying energy does seem overly complex. like there's an argument to having it just be 1gbrree, but that seems weird
[2018-03-26 13:17] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): 1:Gold::Green::Blue::Red::Energy:.
[2018-03-26 13:17] masn6811 (Masne98888): the integer division thing is also still weird. there's an argument for just making it 1gre per, instead of getting it to give 2 at a time
[2018-03-26 13:17] masn6811 (Masne98888): if you pay the engi, it becomes a tesla coil variant
[2018-03-26 13:17] masn6811 (Masne98888): and likely compares badly to it
[2018-03-26 13:18] masn6811 (Masne98888): if you copy the templating from vore/tesla, you cant go too wrong balancewise, if the numbers are right. tech fixing is that OP
[2018-03-26 13:18] masn6811 (Masne98888): but my UI concern still stands
[2018-03-26 13:18] masn6811 (Masne98888): prismata, as you may have noticed, does not have a multi-select system
[2018-03-26 13:19] masn6811 (Masne98888): units such as chill and sniping all target exactly 1 unit
[2018-03-26 13:19] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Can't units be selected by Sweeping?
[2018-03-26 13:20] masn6811 (Masne98888): you can do a mass drone click via it, but that's to use the :Click: ability in multiple copies of the same unit
[2018-03-26 13:20] masn6811 (Masne98888): every :Click: ability itself has 0 or 1 targets
[2018-03-26 13:21] masn6811 (Masne98888): I know your unit is legendary, but it has to not break the game in unlimited supply mode, and a multi-target unit would break shift click
[2018-03-26 13:21] masn6811 (Masne98888): compared to shiftclicking say cryo ray to freeze a dgrid, which works perfectly fine
[2018-03-26 13:22] masn6811 (Masne98888): anyways, bouncing for now. catch you later, or not so later. depends on how im feeling today

[2018-03-27 00:01] redzonex (Redzone): Phoenix (14GGGBRR, 1 supply)
4 HP, Blocker :Blocker:, Fragile :Fragile: 
At the start of your turn, gain :Attack: :Attack: :Attack:.
When Phoenix dies, construct a Phoenix and 4 Gauss Charges with buildtime :Time: 3.
[2018-03-27 00:05] iminabearsuit: Could technically break SDR, if opponent has something like an infusion grid and a tarsier left. And you have this and an animus left.
[2018-03-27 00:05] redzonex (Redzone): Yeah, I'm beginning to realize
[2018-03-27 00:05] http404error: Yeah, your opponent definitely doesn't ever want to kill this, right?
[2018-03-27 00:05] http404error: Right, they don't have a choice
[2018-03-27 00:06] http404error: Which means they won't build attackers?
[2018-03-27 00:06] iminabearsuit: Well they take 3 less damage for three turns. Which usually makes up for the cluster.
[2018-03-27 00:06] http404error: The Phoenix gets buildtime also?
[2018-03-27 00:06] http404error: Hahaha that makes a lot more sense
[2018-03-27 00:07] http404error: Oh yeah, congrats @iminaBearSuit
[2018-03-27 00:07] http404error: Who's this other @iminaBearSuit
[2018-03-27 00:07] iminabearsuit: Uh. Old account, from before the Prismata takeover.
[2018-03-27 00:08] redzonex (Redzone): Yeah I don't think prismata likes recursive units as a design principle
[2018-03-27 00:08] http404error: I have to remember which one to tag when you're offline and can't compare the profile pics
[2018-03-27 00:08] iminabearsuit: I'll try to remember to kick him out once I get to pc again
[2018-03-27 00:08] redzonex (Redzone): I could make it come back with less health each time, but then it becomes rather complex
[2018-03-27 00:09] iminabearsuit: It just seems like a hard unit to balance. Like either you never want it to die like a borehole. Or it breaks SDR.
[2018-03-27 00:10] redzonex (Redzone): Yeah I was trying to fiddle around with numbers to make it an interesting decision
[2018-03-27 00:13] iminabearsuit: The current one is close. But the additional cluster kind of makes it weird.
[2018-03-27 00:15] redzonex (Redzone): i started thinking of it as being used as a big scorchilla
[2018-03-27 00:15] redzonex (Redzone): less efficient than just leaving it up to fire for three turns, but maybe stronger if you have something to sync it with
[2018-03-27 00:15] redzonex (Redzone): and sometimes you'll just need to defend with it
[2018-03-27 00:17] masn6811 (Masne98888): you could just add a click ability to immolate it yourself
[2018-03-27 00:20] .bky_1556 (bky): As far as SDR compliance goes, I think it's okay to have death effects like this:
[2018-03-27 00:20] .bky_1556 (bky): Tortoise (6R, supply 10) 2 Health
Blocker
Start of turn: Gain 1 Attack.
When Tortoise dies, construct a Husk with Chill 1. (It's frozen and can't block)
[2018-03-27 00:20] .bky_1556 (bky): where the dead unit dominates the unit it becomes.
[2018-03-27 00:22] masn6811 (Masne98888): if its not the case, then you can always just add a click ability to suicide it (though you'd have to expedite the death effect by 1 turn, since damage happens on your opponent's turn)
[2018-03-27 00:23] .bky_1556 (bky): If you made the delayed phoenix dominated by the living phoenix i.e. it's vulnerable and dies for real, you could pull it off.
[2018-03-27 00:24] .bky_1556 (bky): Oh, and the gauss charges would need to be no more attack than a 'woke phoenix, and come in before the phoenix does.
[2018-03-27 00:24] .bky_1556 (bky): Tortoise would still work if it made a gauss charge when it died.

[2018-03-27 03:05] .jrkirby (jrkirby): It's not meant to be a "strong attacker" but it's got many uses. Cheap threat, low defensive supply counter, flexible sword ramp for sword costing units, and late game pressure near breaching.

You opponent can "just get an engineer" to block it's attack, but that engineer, but the attack appears before the gold, so they have to buy the engineer first.

But it may be too weak overall at 2R, and perhaps 1R is a more suitable cost. That would put it in line with cryo ray's power level, I think.
